PR surface: Source +1737, Tests +602, Docs +30. Total +2369 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a PR adding QA coverage, and the review findings are source-reproducible from the PR diff.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Author or maintainer should fix the coverage semantics and docs contradiction before merge; the correct baseline meaning may need an owner decision.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): expand live QA coverage
target.body:
## Summary

Expands the WhatsApp live QA lane from a small smoke set into a broad regression lane that covers the extension's main channel capabilities after updates.

This PR also updates the WhatsApp QA driver and gateway-facing send surface so the live lane can exercise structured WhatsApp-specific sends instead of relying on private test-only hooks.

## What Changed

- Adds WhatsApp QA driver/send support for structured sends:
  - contact cards
  - locations
  - stickers
- Adds QA gateway/live-lane helpers for:
  - `send`
  - `poll`
  - `message.action`
  - workspace media fixtures
  - observed-message recording and post-send assertions
- Adds deterministic mock-provider support for WhatsApp long-final responses so chunk/final-message behavior can be asserted reliably.
- Updates WhatsApp auto-reply test harness mocks to implement the expanded listener send surface.

## New WhatsApp QA Coverage

The WhatsApp QA catalog now covers 35 scenarios total.

New scenario families added in this branch include:

- Basic DM/group smoke and gating:
  - canary
  - pairing gate
  - mention gating
  - top-level reply shape
  - restart/resume
- Native command UX:
  - `/help`
  - `/status`
  - `/commands`
  - `/tools compact`
  - `/whoami`
  - `/context list`
  - `/new`
- Usage/final-output behavior:
  - tool-only usage footer
  - long streamed final message accounting
  - long quoted reply chunking
- Reply/quote behavior:
  - reply-to mode quotes the triggering message
  - fresh sends do not reuse prior quote context
- Inbound media/structured messages:
  - image caption
  - audio preflight transcript
  - document caption
  - poll
  - location
  - contact send probe
  - sticker send probe
  - group audio gating
- Outbound send/action behavior:
  - image
  - document
  - audio/voice
  - multi-media
  - document filename preservation
  - native poll
  - message.action react
  - message.action upload-file
- Access control:
  - DM open
  - DM disabled
  - group open
  - group disabled
  - group allowlist block
- Native approvals:
  - exec allow-once
  - exec deny
  - exec reaction approval
  - plugin allow-once
- Status reactions:
  - observable WhatsApp status reactions

## Default Lane Behavior

- `live-frontier` remains intentionally small at 7 default scenarios for fast live smoke coverage.
- `mock-openai` now runs 28 deterministic WhatsApp scenarios by default.
- Approval and some heavier/blocking scenarios remain explicit-only unless selected by scenario ID.

The mocked-provider scenarios still run through the real WhatsApp transport; only the model/provider response is mocked so the lane can assert exact markers, chunk counts, and structured behavior without frontier-model variance.

## Verification

Passed locally after rebasing onto `origin/main`:

- `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/qa-driver.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `git diff --check`

Passed via Blacksmith Testbox:

- `pnpm check:changed`
